reasoning: The target federal funds range was 4.25%–4.50% at the end of 2024. As of November 2025, the Fed has only cut rates by 25 basis points in 2025 (in September). The market consensus expects at most 3-4 quarter-point cuts for the entire calendar year, with the median FOMC dot plot at 3.50%–3.75%. Eight cuts would equate to a total of 200 basis points of easing, which is physically impossible to reach in 2025 without highly unlikely, unprecedented emergency inter-meeting cuts of ~175 basis points in the final month of the year given current economic outlooks (steady GDP growth and 4.5% unemployment). Therefore, it is virtually certain there will not be 8+ rate cuts in 2025.
